The, the, I guess, ideal way for working with GrowthX is that what you're describing is having a totally unique and or quote unquote proprietary perspective on something that would allow it to really differentiate from the other 10 articles on the first page of Google.
Okay.
Yeah.
So in order to do that, there's.
There's only a couple of ways really that you can do that.
Number one, the traditional way is that you have somebody internally in the company that gets a piece of content or even earlier gets a concept of what they want to write about.
And they say in an outline, they say, this is my perspective, and this is how I want you to form this concept.
And then the piece gets written based off of this perspective that is either pre-given or given after it's been written and the whole thing is tweaked.
That's the traditional way.
The now more like, quote unquote, advanced way that's still being formulated, by the way, companies like GrowthX and Aerox and other things like that, they're still figuring this out.
This is actually the holy grail, because if you want really high quality content, as you know, it has to be like EAT, right?
One of those is expert, like you want expert insights into your piece.
And that's what Google, but also the LLMs love.
But it's extremely hard to figure out how to do that in a scalable way.
So- Yeah.
One of the ways that they have played around with is having the same way that we have an artifact for your company context in your products and services and so forth.
You would have an additional basically chunk of information that would be expert knowledge from the company.
That does take typically a little bit more engineering and structuring to do this.
But I'll give a very concrete example.

So if the business, for instance, has a founder who is like super active on social and does a lot of content, could we basically take all of that content and put it into a reservoir, which then gets plugged into our pipeline?
And every time we're creating content, it's looking at that and seeing, is there any specific perspective that, for instance, Jordan, let's say, has on this thing?
And we want to make sure that we pull that, right?
So if you have lots of video content or X posts.
Or whatever it is, that can be a really good source.
Another good source is if a company has a ton of internal documentation where, you know, it's the founder talking internally to like, this is how we do things.
And this is what's different about us and blah, blah, blah, blah, right?
If they have a huge library of that, that's another option.

The third option, which is a little less good but still can be helpful, is if they have like a really big like Zendesk, like help desk thing where they're really going deep into not just how to do something but why they do it the way they do.
That can also be really helpful.
Okay.
So those are kind of the two primary ways that you can do it.
